leg: Qwen3-30B seq512 — THIRD dose point, tests the fill->gap CURVE SHAPE
authorized: 2026-07-11 by Jordan ('add the seq 512 point to test the curve shape')
host: RunPod SECURE RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell WS (sm_120, 96GB), pod x5274jeaqof7w1, driver 580; memlock gate waived (false gate)
design: IDENTICAL to the decisive leg: warm-up throwaway + 3 warm whole floor arms + 1 routed(+STAGEDCNT), Qwen3-30B, seq 512, sample_packing, seed 42, prediction_loss_only, 150 steps, eval_steps 50. Floor = spread of 3 whole finals; PASS iff |routed - mean(whole)| <= spread.

x_coordinate_is_MEASURED_not_assumed.packing_caveat: Phase-0 is UNRELIABLE under sample_packing: the hi leg predicted rf 0.796 (fill 0.204) at seq2048 but MEASURED 0.97 (fill 0.03) because packing diversifies routing. seq256 happened to match (0.687 pred ~= 0.688 meas). So seq512 MEASURED rf will likely land ABOVE 0.73 (fill BELOW 0.27), probably ~0.78-0.85 (fill ~0.15-0.22).
x_coordinate_is_MEASURED_not_assumed.rule: the STAGEDCNT-measured mean rf is the x-coordinate. The shape test is evaluated at whatever fill is measured, NOT the prediction.

power_law_sqrt_ish.note: convex-down (fast rise then flatten); fit exponent 0.469 ~ sqrt

if_fill_0.27.verdict: WEAK — too close to seq256, laws barely separate

if_fill_0.15.verdict: GOOD — laws separate by ~18x the floor

shape_verdict_rule: place the measured (fill, gap). If gap is closer to the POWER prediction -> convex/saturating dose-response (marginal fill matters less as fill grows). If closer to LINEAR -> constant marginal cost per fill unit. If fill lands >=0.25, report the point but flag the shape test as low-power (predictions too close).
cost_budget: ~$6-8 (5 arms; seq512 evals are ~2x seq256's per-batch but ~half the batch count -> similar wall).
scope: Third point tests SHAPE. The DIRECTION (H_FILL) is already settled (da6e776); this does not re-litigate it.
